steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: For a system of two linear equations to have infinitely many solutions, the equations must be identical — same slope and same y-intercept. Find the values of $m$ and $b$ that make $y = mx + b$ identical to $y = \frac{2}{5}x + 2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Matching $y = mx + b$ to $y = \frac{2}{5}x + 2$ requires $m = \frac{2}{5}$ and $b = 2$.
  • Choice A — '$m = \frac{5}{2},\ b = 2$': wrong slope — this is the reciprocal, not equal. ✗
  • Choice B — '$m = \frac{2}{5},\ b = 0$': slope matches but y-intercept doesn't. ✗
  • Choice C — '$m = 2,\ b = \frac{2}{5}$': both values swapped. ✗
  • Choice D — '$m = \frac{2}{5},\ b = 2$': both values match exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
